## Summary

Fix a preexisting test failure in `reviseCardContract.test.ts` that has been blocking CI on every PR. The test "routes cards to needs_targeting when A/B/C share repetitive lead-in wording" expected a "materially distinct" rejection but got "Candidate A is not copy-paste ready" because the test data contained meta-editorial patterns.

## Scope

**Touches:** `__tests__/lib/revision/reviseCardContract.test.ts` — replaced 3 candidate text strings in the shared-lead-in test case.

**Does NOT touch:** Any source code, production logic, or other test files.

## Tests Updated

The failing test now passes (15/15 in the suite). The candidate texts were rewritten to use manuscript-quality prose that passes the `candidateTextIsCopyPasteReady` check while still sharing the 4-token lead-in "Move aside, Small Fry, Twillow" to exercise the `sharedLeadingTokenCount` code path.

**Root cause:** The original candidates contained phrases flagged by `META_EDITORIAL_PATTERNS` (e.g. "answers in motion", "a physical beat carries", "pressure stays visible"), which were added after the test was written. The copy-paste-ready check fires before the shared-lead-in check, changing the rejection reason.

## Risks & Anomalies

Zero risk — test-only change, no production code modified.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

N/A — no user input handling.

## Internal Process Leakage

N/A — no process details exposed.

## Input → Action → Output

1. Test provides 3 candidate texts sharing a 4-token lead-in prefix
2. `validateReviseCardContract()` passes copy-paste-ready check (fixed), then detects shared lead-in
3. Returns `{ readiness: 'needs_targeting', reason: 'Candidate options are not materially distinct.' }`

N/A — test infrastructure only.

N/A — no new routes, jobs, or pipeline changes.

N/A — test-only change.
